Based on recent medical studies reviewed, Kraissl's lines indeed correspond primarily to points where collagen fibers relax and become taut less frequently than other parts of the body surface. They act almost identically to sagittal folds seen commonly appearing on upper thighs due reduced muscular activity resulting in minimally stressed zones making ideal locations for surgical incisions aiming to avoid excessive scarring post-operation.
Key findings confirm that Kraissl's lines indicate places characterized by low tensile forces normally exerted on cutaneous surfaces allowing easier passage during operations thus reducing adverse Based on recent medical studies reviewed, Kraissl's lines indeed correspond primarily to points where collagen fibers relax and become taut less frequently than other parts of the body surface. They act almost identically to sagittal folds seen commonly appearing on upper thighs due reduced muscular activity resulting in minimally stressed zones making ideal locations for surgical incisions aiming to avoid excessive scarring post-operation.
